stem: An engineer takes 30 measurements of noise level using a noise meter. The measurements have a mean of 120.3 dB and a standard deviation of 13.9 dB. However, calibration tests reveal that a noise meter systematically overestimates the true value by between 4.3% and 7.2%. Estimate the true noise level and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 120.3 dB. After correcting for the 5.8% bias: ≈ 113.7 dB. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (4.3%-7.2%) and sampling variability: [107.1, 120.4]. Point estimate: 113.7 dB.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
